metadata.title: Straight Into Canvas — AI+1 series 4/4
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent; callout ink #161511 on cream. Do not retint.
metadata.greeting_note: Italian short form (HAI budget) — HAI takes only the shortest forms (Hi · Ola · Hej · Ciao); never spell out HumanitariansAI in the greeting (the footer chip does that). Wagwan n/a (Bear only).
metadata.purpose: CUSTOMER pitch, not investor pitch: sell custom workshops, custom courses, and $1 Kindle books from a nonprofit doing good, and send viewers to the Humanitarians AI YouTube.
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk. SPARK-LINE LAW: every inner beat carries a short serif spark line. HANDOFF LAW: 2nd-to-last beat is the composer with a paste-ready action, greeting 'Your turn.'. OUTRO LAW: restate the title, poster serif, terracotta period. House grammar: >=1 PREDICT beat; verdict page carries the phase gate; decisions.json + DECISIONS.md ship beside this sheet.

beats.narration_text: The part that stuck with me is that the .imscc cartridge is a file I own that keeps running even if you disappear tomorrow. I teach on Canvas and want to pressure-test that no-lock-in claim. Walk me through: (1) exactly what happens after I upload and confirm the cartridge — which modules, videos, and exams land where; (2) how a white-label workshop keeps my brand on the front while your instructors build the material; (3) what could actually break my course a year out if the rights truly travel with the file.

props.command: The part that stuck with me is that the .imscc cartridge is a file I own that keeps running even if you disappear tomorrow. I teach on Canvas and want to pressure-test that no-lock-in claim. Walk me through: (1) exactly what happens after I upload and confirm the cartridge — which modules, videos, and exams land where; (2) how a white-label workshop keeps my brand on the front while your instructors build the material; (3) what could actually break my course a year out if the rights truly travel with the file.

beats.narration_text: The format is dot I M S C C — Canvas's own import format. No integration project, no I T ticket queue, no vendor review to schedule. Upload, confirm, teach.
If you want humans in the room, we deliver custom training and workshops ourselves — your team, your cases, our instructors.
Or white-label it: we build the workshop, your experts deliver it, and your brand carries it. Your bench gets the material. Your clients see you.
Now predict the total lock-in here — the platform fee, the seat license, the annual renewal. Count it up. Take a second.

Zero. The cartridge is a file you own. The zip is a folder you own. The rights travel with them. If we disappeared tomorrow, your course keeps running — which is exactly how course material should work.
Let's recap with Claude. One page. A cartridge into Canvas, training if you want us, white-label if you want you — and files you own either way.

artifactLines: .imscc export → direct Canvas upload: modules, videos, exams standing in place.
Custom training and live workshops, delivered by Humanitarians AI.
White-label: we build it, your experts deliver it, your brand carries it.
Hiring us funds a 501(c)(3): fellows, mentorship, and free AI education.
Phase gate: files you own · no platform, no seats, no renewal · your course outlives us.

beats.narration_text: Your turn — the day the zip lands, paste this. Ten minutes later you're announcing week one.

props.command: When my AI+1 zip arrives: open the .imscc, list exactly what will land in Canvas — modules, videos, exams — and draft my week-one announcement to students.
